title: Verisk Analytics (VRSK) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Discounted cash flow valuation for Verisk Analytics modeling strong free cash flow generation driven by recurring subscription revenue and an entrenched insurance data ecosystem. Economic Prospect Score: 85/100 — Strong Prospect.

revenueGrowth.rationale: An 8.0% growth rate models consistent structural demand for proprietary data analytics, aided by consistent pricing leverage and product expansions into cyber and climate risks.

terminalGrowthRate.rationale: A 3.0% long-term growth rate reflects Verisk's entrenched position, growing slightly faster than GDP due to the increasing complexity of risk pricing.

discountRate.rationale: An 8.0% discount rate reflects Verisk's very low business risk profile, highly predictable revenue base, and robust recurring cash flows.

prospectVerdictDetail: Verisk Analytics benefits from a highly durable, data-driven moat characterized by intense network effects and immense switching costs. Its proprietary databases are virtually indispensable to the P&C insurance industry, leading to robust profit margins (29.6%). With predictable, subscription-like revenue and strong capital returns, it commands a very strong competitive position.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Excellent pricing power and highly embedded product suite.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Verisk exhibits steady, albeit not hyper-growth, top-line performance. Because its products are mission-critical for regulatory compliance and risk assessment in insurance, growth remains resilient even during economic downturns.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: Verisk is essentially the industry standard for property and casualty (P&C) insurance data analytics. Its market share is overwhelmingly dominant, and it faces minimal direct competition capable of offering an equivalent historical dataset.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: The company commands extraordinary pricing power. Its data feeds are a negligible fraction of an insurer's total costs but are fundamental to their underwriting accuracy, allowing Verisk to implement consistent, unquestioned price increases.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Verisk continually innovates by expanding its predictive models to cover emerging risks like climate change and cyber liabilities. However, the core business relies heavily on its legacy, established databases rather than rapid technological pivots.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Impenetrable barriers to entry driven by an irreplaceable proprietary dataset.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Insurers hardwire Verisk's data into their core underwriting and claims processing algorithms. Unwinding these integrations and switching to an unproven alternative carries catastrophic operational and financial risk for clients.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Verisk possesses a powerful data network effect. Insurers contribute their own claims data to Verisk in exchange for industry-wide analytics. More participants yield richer data, improving predictive models and further locking in clients.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Verisk's historical datasets, spanning decades, are functionally impossible to replicate. This proprietary intellectual property acts as a definitive barrier against new entrants lacking historical data density.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: As a data and software provider, Verisk operates a capital-light model. The marginal cost of delivering its data to a new client is near zero, generating immense free cash flow and high returns on equity.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Viewed as a defensive growth staple, though valuation multiples remain demanding.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Analysts consistently revise estimates upward due to the predictable, recurring nature of Verisk's subscription revenue. Surprises are rare, but the stability is highly valued by institutional investors.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The market views Verisk as an elite 'compounder' stock. The narrative centers on its defensive characteristics and ability to capitalize on increasing global risk complexities, like extreme weather events.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management has successfully divested non-core assets (like its energy and financial services units) to focus purely on the higher-margin, higher-growth insurance sector. They also aggressively return capital via share repurchases.

prospectKeyRisks: Consolidation within the insurance industry could reduce its overall customer base and pressure pricing leverage.
Data privacy regulations (like GDPR or CCPA) could limit the scope of personal data Verisk can collect and monetize.
A catastrophic cybersecurity breach compromising its proprietary database would severely damage client trust.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Increasing frequency of extreme weather events driving higher demand for advanced climate modeling data.
Expansion of predictive analytics into specialized, high-growth insurance lines like cyber risk and liability.
Continued margin expansion following the divestiture of its lower-margin non-insurance business segments.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored VRSK at 82/100 and Opus at 86/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
